get.description:
Returns a list of transactions that exist on the Stellar network.  Provides general information such as: identifier and hash of transaction,  sequence number of the ledger and protocol version, encoded body,  meta and result of transaction, ledger timestamp in UNIX format.

Available types for filtering the list:

Operation type by group name

`payments` - Group payments including operations: CreateAccount, AccountMerge,  Payment, PathPaymentStrictReceive, PathPaymentStrictSend, CreateClaimableBalance,  ClaimClaimableBalance, Clawback, ClawbackClaimableBalance, Inflation

`trustlines` - Group trustlines including operations: ChangeTrust, AllowTrust,  SetTrustLineFlags

`dex` - Group DEX trading including operations: ManageSellOffer, ManageBuyOffer,  CreatePassiveSellOffer, LiquidityPoolDeposit, LiquidityPoolWithdraw

`settings` - Group account settings including operations: CreateAccount, SetOptions,  ChangeTrust, AllowTrust, AccountMerge, Inflation, ManageData, BumpSequence,  BeginSponsoringFutureReserves, EndSponsoringFutureReserves, RevokeSponsorship,  SetTrustLineFlags

`soroban` - Group smart contracts including operations: InvokeHostFunction,  BumpFootprintExpiration, RestoreFootprint

Numeric operation type code

`0` - CreateAccount
`1` - Payment
`2` - PathPaymentStrictReceive
`3` - ManageSellOffer
`4` - CreatePassiveSellOffer
`5` - SetOptions
`6` - ChangeTrust
`7` - AllowTrust
`8` - AccountMerge
`9` - Inflation
`10` - ManageData
`11` - BumpSequence
`12` - ManageBuyOffer
`13` - PathPaymentStrictSend
`14` - CreateClaimableBalance
`15` - ClaimClaimableBalance
`16` - BeginSponsoringFutureReserves
`17` - EndSponsoringFutureReserves
`18` - RevokeSponsorship
`19` - Clawback
`20` - ClawbackClaimableBalance
`21` - SetTrustLineFlags
`22` - LiquidityPoolDeposit
`23` - LiquidityPoolWithdraw
`24` - InvokeHostFunction
`25` - BumpFootprintExpiration
`26` - RestoreFootprint

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Applies a filter by the operation source account list (up to 50 per request)

parameters.name: destination
parameters.description: Applies a filter by the operation destination account list (up to 50 per request)

parameters.name: src_asset
parameters.description: Applies a filter by the operation source asset list (up to 50 per request)

parameters.name: dest_asset
parameters.description: Applies a filter by the operation destination asset list (up to 50 per request)

parameters.name: type

get.description:
Returns a list of assets that exist on Stellar network. It support searching by asset code, issuer, home domain, and metadata from associated `stellar.toml` file.

Possible sorting options:

- `rating` - composite asset rating derived from on-chain stats
- `created` - asset age
- `payments` - total number of payments
- `trades` - total number of trades
- `trustlines` - number of established trustlines
- `volume` - overall trading volume
- `volume7d` - weekly trading volume

Provides information about soroban contract address,  current price with dynamics over 7 days, total number of trades/payments,  overall trades/payments volume, total supply, trustlines established to an asset,  rating and metadata from associated `stellar.toml` file. get.description:
To solve the problem of assets ranking on StellarExpert, we designed a system of technical indicators based purely on the ledger activity, including

  - asset age (time elapsed sisnce the first payment)
  - established trustlines
  - total number of payments
  - total number of trades
  - weekly trading volume
  - market liquidity (based on the relative slippage of {ASSET}/XLM market)
  - asset interoperability (based on additional asset metadata and supported SEP standards)

All indicators are calculated on the logarithmic scale to normalize distribution for assets with high trading/transfer activity. The compound rating calculated as the average of all seven indicators can be used to roughly estimate the popularity of any Stellar asset purely from the technical point of view.

get.description:
Returns the list of directory entries with paging navigation links. Optionally filtered by address, tag, or arbitrary search string. This API endpoint follows Stellar Horizon API response format convention. A response result contains records and navigation links.

#### Query examples:
Fetch information about multiple accounts in one call

`curl -g "https://api.stellar.expert/explorer/directory?address[]=GA6HCMBLTZS5VYYBCATRBRZ3BZJMAFUDKYYF6AH6MVCMGWMRDNSWJPIH&address[]=GAP5LETOV6YIE62YAM56STDANPRDO7ZFDBGSNHJQIYGGKSMOZAHOOS2S"`

Search for Kraken's deposit account

`curl "https://api.stellar.expert/explorer/directory?search=kraken"`

Find accounts tagged as malicious or unsafe

`curl -g "https://api.stellar.expert/explorer/directory?tag[]=malicious&tag[]=unsafe"`

Lookup addresses reported for "staking"-related scams

`curl -g "https://api.stellar.expert/explorer/directory?tag[]=malicious&search=stacking"`

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: Applies a filter by the addresses list (up to 50 per request)

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Applies a filter by tags

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Applies a full-test search filter by an entry address, name, or domain

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: An address from which to continue search (refers to the `paging_token` value from a results set)

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Results sorting order

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Results data page size

example.error: Bad request. Invalid "tag" parameter value. Unknown tag.
Bad request. Invalid "search" parameter value. Expected a string (3-60 characters).

Returns a list of markets that exist on the Stellar network.  Provides general information about asset pair, such as:  base and quote volume for 7 days/24 hours, price for 7 days  and total number of trades for 24 hours.

Possible type filter:

`all` - returns all existing markets
`xlm` - returns all xml markets
`crypto` - returns all markets with asset type crypto
`fiat` - returns all markets with asset type fiat
`other` - returns all other markets, except xlm, crypto and fiat markets

Possible sorting options:

`created` - date of market creation
`trades7d` - total number of trades for 7 days period 
`trades24h` - total number of trades for last 24 hour

This API endpoint follows Stellar Horizon API response format convention.  A response result contains records and navigation links.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Type filter

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Results sorting parameter

example.error: Bad request. Invalid parameter: "network". Unknown network name.
Bad request. Invalid parameter: "asset". Invalid asset descriptor. Use {code}-{issuer}-{type} format or contract address.

get.description: Returns a list of selling/buying assets that exist on the Stellar network.  Provides general information about asset pair, such as: base and quote  volume for 7 days/24 hours, total number of trades for 24 hours.

parameters.name: selling
parameters.description: An asset in the format {CODE}-{ISSUER} (or "XLM" for Stellar lumens)

parameters.name: buying
parameters.description: An asset in the format {CODE}-{ISSUER} (or "XLM" for Stellar lumens)

example.error: Bad request. Invalid parameter: "network". Unknown network name.
Bad request. Invalid parameter: "selling". Invalid asset descriptor. Use {code}-{issuer}-{type} format or contract address.
Bad request. Invalid parameter: "buying". Invalid asset descriptor. Use {code}-{issuer}-{type} format or contract address.
Not found. Market statistics were not found on the ledger. Check if you specified the assets correctly.

example.error: Bad request. Invalid parameter: "network". Unknown network name.

get.description: Returns general details for a given offer, identified by offerId.  Provides the following information: which account created/updated  the offer and when, selling/buying pair, total number of trades  for this offer, the offer amount and price.

example.error: Bad request. Invalid parameter: "network". Unknown network name.
Not found. Offer with current id was not found.

get.description:
Returns the trades history for a given offer identifier.  Provides the following information: buyer/seller account,  transaction identifier, transaction asset pair with their  amount and price for the timestamp.

This API endpoint follows Stellar Horizon API response format convention.  A response result contains records and navigation links.

example.error: Bad request. Invalid parameter: "network". Unknown network name.

get.description:
Returns aggregated asset price history for any period in time (controlled by `from` and `to` parameters). It's possible to adjust data granularity using `resolution` parameter. When omitted, resolutions is automatically calculated based on the requested period to fit up to 200 records.
Note: Selected range (`from`..`to`) takes precedence over the `resolution` parameter. If the estimated number of records with a given resolution for a particular time range exceeds 200, it is readjusted automatically.
This API endpoint returns the data in the following format:
[`TIMESTAMP`,`OPEN`,`LOW`,`HIGH`,`CLOSE`,`QUOTE_VOLUME`,`BASE_VOLUME`,`TRADES`]
(Timeframe UNIX timestamp, Open price, Maximum price within timeframe, Minimum price within timeframe, Close price,  Quote Volume, Base volume, Trades count).

example.error: Bad request. Invalid parameter: "network". Unknown network name.
Bad request. Invalid parameter: "asset". Invalid asset descriptor. Use {code}-{issuer}-{type} format or contract address.
Bad request. Parameter "to" should be larger than "from".

get.description: Returns aggregated data [TIMESTAMP, OPEN, LOW, HIGH,  CLOSE, QUOTE_VOLUME, BASE_VOLUME, TRADES_COUNT] of  market asset pair that exist on the Stellar network.
